mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent above-target inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payroll employment (PAYEMS > 100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals a tightening labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through corporate earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or individual series show no significant correlation with SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST > 900k) alongside expanding GDP (GDPC1 > 2%) indicates strong demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual-proxy signal underperforms a single-proxy benchmark by > 15% in rolling 5-year tests, or returns turn negative when either series falls below thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO < 90) signal weakening supply chain capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in > 50% of periods where INDPRO falls below 90, or the signal's IC exceeds -0.07 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) create headwinds for equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX returns exceed 1.5% in 60%+ of instances when FEDFUNDS surpasses 4.5%, or the signal's IC remains above -0.05 in 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Surging consumer spending (RSAFS > 405k) reflects robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through increased corporate revenues.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain negative in > 40% of periods where RSAFS exceeds 405k, or the signal's IC drops below 0.04 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 900k) combined with rising GDP (GDPC1 > 2%) indicate a synchronized demand surge, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal underperforms standalone proxies by > 20% in rolling 5-year tests, or returns turn negative when either series falls below thresholds.
